Class ExpenseLoggingScreen

java.lang.Object
ui.BaseScreen
ui.ExpenseLoggingScreen
public class ExpenseLoggingScreen extends BaseScreen
Screen that allows the user to log a new expense.

Collects an amount in EGP and a category selection, validates the inputs, then delegates to App.logExpense(double, int) before returning to the dashboard.

  • Method Details

    • loadCategories

      private void loadCategories()
      Populates the category combo box with all categories from the database and syncs selectedCategory to the first item.

    • initComponents

      protected void initComponents()
      Initialises all UI components (fields, buttons, labels, etc.). Called before BaseScreen.initLayout().
      Specified by:
      initComponents in class BaseScreen

    • initLayout

      protected void initLayout()
      Arranges the initialised components into the BaseScreen.panel hierarchy. Called after BaseScreen.initComponents().
      Specified by:
      initLayout in class BaseScreen

    • validateInputs

      public boolean validateInputs()
      Validates all form inputs before saving. Shows an inline error and returns false on the first failure.
      Returns:
      true if amount is a positive number and a category is selected

    • showError

      public void showError(String message)
      Shows a validation error message below the form.
      Parameters:
      message - the error text to display

    • onCategorySelected

      public void onCategorySelected(Category category)
      Updates the selected category when the combo box selection changes.
      Parameters:
      category - the newly selected Category

    • showSuccessAndReturn

      public void showSuccessAndReturn()
      Navigates back to the dashboard after a successful save.

    • onSaveClicked

      public void onSaveClicked()
      Handles the "Save" button click. Validates inputs, logs the expense, recalculates the daily limit, and returns to the dashboard.
